collocations.en: Have you ever been to Paris?

patterns.title: have been to vs have gone to
patterns.explain: have been to 表示「去过某地且已返回」；have gone to 表示「去了某地（人现在不在此处）」。
examples: I have been to London twice.（我去过伦敦两次。）
He has gone to Beijing.（他去北京了，不在这里。）

collocations.en: He has gone to the office.

patterns.title: have gone to vs have been to
patterns.explain: have gone to 强调人已离开说话地点；have been to 强调经历，人已回来。
examples: She has gone to the shops.（她去商店了。）
I have never been to Japan.（我从未去过日本。）

patterns.title: as soon as 时间状语从句
patterns.explain: as soon as 引导时间从句，主句和从句的时态需配合：将来主句用一般现在时代替将来时。
examples: As soon as he arrived, he began to work.
I'll call you as soon as I get home.

collocations.en: in spite of + 名词/动名词

patterns.title: in spite of vs although
patterns.explain: in spite of 后接名词或动名词；although 后接从句。二者都表示让步。
examples: In spite of the rain, they went out.
In spite of being tired, she kept working.

collocations.cn: 不妨做某事；还是做某事吧

collocations.en: You might as well tell me.

patterns.title: might as well do
patterns.explain: 表示在没有更好选择时「不妨做某事」，语气较随意。
examples: We might as well wait here.
You might as well go by bus.

patterns.title: would rather do than do
patterns.explain: would rather 后接动词原形；than 后也用原形，表示偏好选择。
examples: I would rather stay at home.
She would rather walk than take a bus.

patterns.title: had better（无变位）
patterns.explain: had better 中 had 不随人称变化，后接动词原形；否定用 had better not do。
examples: You had better hurry up.
We had better not be late.

collocations.en: be going to + 动词原形

patterns.title: be going to vs will
patterns.explain: be going to 表计划或有迹象的将来；will 表临时决定或单纯预测。
examples: We are going to visit Australia next month.
Look at the clouds — it's going to rain.

collocations.cn: 过去成功做到（特定场合）

patterns.title: be able to vs can
patterns.explain: can 只有现在和过去式 could；be able to 可用于各种进行时和完成时，表「成功做到」。
examples: He was able to escape from the fire.
I haven't been able to finish it yet.

patterns.title: want + 名词 / want to do
patterns.explain: want 后接名词或 to do；接人作宾语时用 want sb to do。不接动名词。
examples: I want a cup of tea.
She wants to learn English.

patterns.title: must vs have to / mustn't vs don't have to
patterns.explain: must 表主观必须或肯定推测；mustn't 表禁止；don't have to 表不必。
examples: You must finish your homework.
You mustn't smoke here.

patterns.title: already vs yet
patterns.explain: already 用于肯定句，放 have 之后；yet 用于否定句和疑问句，表「还（未）」。
examples: I have already finished.
Have you finished yet?

patterns.title: yet 的位置
patterns.explain: yet 常用于现在完成时的否定句和疑问句句末。
examples: He hasn't arrived yet.
Have you seen it yet?

patterns.title: for / since 与完成时
patterns.explain: since 接时间点（1990, last week）；for 接时间段（two years）。均与现在完成时连用。
examples: I have lived here since 2010.
She has been away for three days.

patterns.title: ago 与一般过去时
patterns.explain: ago 从现在往前推算，必须与一般过去时连用，不能用于完成时。
examples: He left a few minutes ago.
The story happened many years ago.

patterns.title: good → better → best
patterns.explain: good/well 的比较级是 better，最高级是 best，均为不规则变化。
examples: This book is better than that one.
She sings better than I do.

patterns.title: interested vs interesting
patterns.explain: interested 修饰人「感到感兴趣的」；interesting 修饰物「有趣的」。
examples: I am interested in history.
This is an interesting book.

patterns.title: interesting vs interested
patterns.explain: -ing 形容词描述事物特征；-ed 形容词描述人的感受。
examples: The film was very interesting.
We were all interested in the news.

collocations.cn: 穿上（动作）vs 穿着（状态）

patterns.title: put on vs wear vs dress
patterns.explain: put on 强调穿的动作；wear 强调穿着的状态；dress 后接人（dress oneself）。
examples: Put on your coat.
She is wearing a red dress.

collocations.en: send sb sth / send sth to sb

patterns.title: send 双宾语结构
patterns.explain: send 可接双宾语：send me a letter = send a letter to me。
examples: He sent me a postcard from Paris.
She sent the package to her friend.

patterns.title: fail to do
patterns.explain: fail to do 表示「未能做到」；cannot fail to do 表示「必定会」。
examples: He failed to arrive on time.
The book cannot fail to interest you.

patterns.title: succeed in + 动名词
patterns.explain: succeed 是不及物动词，in 后接动名词，不能接不定式。
examples: They succeeded in solving the problem.
He succeeded his father as manager.

patterns.title: arrive in/at（不及物）
patterns.explain: arrive 是不及物动词，不能接宾语，不加 to；大地点用 in，小地点用 at。
examples: They arrived in London yesterday.
We arrived at the station early.

patterns.title: promise to do / promise that
patterns.explain: promise 后可接 to do、宾语从句或双宾语。
examples: He promised to help us.
The doctor promised that the man would recover soon.

patterns.title: regret doing vs regret to do
patterns.explain: regret doing 后悔已做的事；regret to do 对将要说的事表示遗憾（regret to inform you）。
examples: I regret telling him the secret.
I regret to say that we cannot help.

collocations.cn: 想知道为什么/什么/如何

patterns.title: wonder + 疑问从句
patterns.explain: wonder 后接 if/whether 或 wh- 从句，表示「想知道」。
examples: I wonder if he is at home.
She wondered why he was late.

patterns.title: realize vs notice
patterns.explain: realize 强调「突然意识到、认识到」；notice 强调「注意到」细节。
examples: He realized his mistake after landing.
I didn't realize how late it was.

patterns.title: avoid + 动名词
patterns.explain: avoid 后接动名词，不接不定式。
examples: He tried to avoid meeting her.
Avoid making the same mistake.

patterns.title: admit + 动名词 / that 从句
patterns.explain: admit 后可接动名词或 that 从句，表示承认。
examples: He admitted making a mistake.
She admitted that she was wrong.

patterns.title: make sb do / be made to do
patterns.explain: make 是使役动词，后接省略 to 的不定式；被动语态须恢复 to：be made to do。
examples: The news made him happy.
He was made to work long hours.

patterns.title: ring vs call
patterns.explain: 英式英语常用 ring (up) sb；美式更常用 call sb。过去式 rang，过去分词 rung。
examples: I'll ring you tonight.
The bell rang at eight o'clock.

patterns.title: call at vs call on
patterns.explain: call at + 地点；call on + 人。英式英语也用 ring 表打电话。
examples: I'll call you later.
He called at the office yesterday.

patterns.title: catch 不规则变化
patterns.explain: catch — caught — caught。catch a bus 表「赶车」，非「抓车」。
examples: I caught the last bus home.
He caught the boy stealing apples.

patterns.title: cost 的主语是物
patterns.explain: cost 主语通常是物或 it，不用人作主语（不说 I cost 100元，说 It cost me 100 yuan）。
examples: The book cost me twenty pounds.
It costs a lot to live in London.

patterns.title: appreciate + 名词/动名词
patterns.explain: appreciate 后接名词或动名词，不接 to do；表「感激」时较正式。
examples: I appreciate your help.
We appreciate having good neighbours.

patterns.title: accept vs receive
patterns.explain: receive 只表示「收到」；accept 表示「接受」，含主观意愿。
examples: He received a gift but did not accept it.
She accepted the job offer.

patterns.title: hide 不规则变化
patterns.explain: hide — hid — hidden。hide 可作及物/不及物动词。
examples: He hid the letter in a drawer.
The child hid behind the door.

patterns.title: wake — woke — woken
patterns.explain: wake up 可作不及物或及物短语；过去式 woke，过去分词 woken（英式）。
examples: I wake up at seven every day.
Please wake me up at six.

patterns.title: such ... that vs so ... that
patterns.explain: such 后接名词短语（such a cold day）；so 后接形容词/副词（so cold that）。
examples: It was such a cold winter that he moved away.
The weather was so wet that he stayed in.

patterns.title: so ... that 结果状语从句
patterns.explain: so 修饰形容词或副词，that 引导结果从句。修饰名词用 such。
examples: The weather was so wet that he hardly left the house.
She spoke so quickly that I couldn't follow.

patterns.title: have something done
patterns.explain: have + 宾语 + 过去分词，表示请别人做某事，本课 She had the walls painted。
examples: She had the walls painted in warm colours.
He had the floor repaired after the fire.

patterns.title: hit sb on/in the + 身体部位
patterns.explain: 打/碰身体某部位用 on/in the + 部位，不用 hit sb's head。
examples: The ball hit him on the head.
He hit the man in the stomach.

patterns.title: suggest + 动名词
patterns.explain: suggest 后接动名词，不接 to do；that 从句中用 should do 或动词原形。
examples: I suggest going by train.
He suggested that we (should) leave early.

patterns.title: finish + 动名词
patterns.explain: finish 后接动名词，不接 to do。
examples: Have you finished reading the book?
She finished her homework early.

patterns.title: stop doing vs stop to do
patterns.explain: stop doing = 停止正在做的事；stop to do = 停下来以便做另一件事。
examples: He stopped smoking.（他戒烟了。）
He stopped to buy a newspaper.（他停下来买报。）

patterns.title: remember doing vs remember to do
patterns.explain: remember doing 回忆已发生的事；remember to do 提醒将来要做的事。
examples: I remember meeting him.（我记得见过他。）
Remember to lock the door.（记得锁门。）

patterns.title: forget doing vs forget to do
patterns.explain: forget to do 忘记尚未做的事；forget doing 较少用，指不记得做过。
examples: Don't forget to post the letter.
I'll never forget visiting Paris.

patterns.title: try to do vs try doing
patterns.explain: try to do 强调努力尝试；try doing 强调试验某种方法。
examples: I tried to open the door.（我试图开门。）
Try using a different key.（试试用另一把钥匙。）

patterns.title: a pair of + 复数名词
patterns.explain: a pair of 后接复数名词，谓语动词可用单数或复数（英式常单数）。
examples: I need a new pair of shoes.
A pair of glasses was on the table.

collocations.en: a lot of + 可数/不可数名词

patterns.title: good vs well
patterns.explain: good 是形容词；well 作副词修饰动词，作形容词时仅表「健康」。
examples: She is good at English.
He speaks English well.

patterns.title: look 短语辨析
patterns.explain: look at 看；look for 找；look after 照顾；look forward to 盼望（to 后接 -ing）。
examples: Look at the blackboard.
I am looking for my keys.

patterns.title: listen vs hear
patterns.explain: listen 强调主动听，须加 to；hear 强调听到声音，不需 to。
examples: Listen to the teacher.
I can hear the music.

patterns.title: hear vs listen
patterns.explain: hear 表感官「听见」；listen to 表有意「倾听」。
examples: I heard a noise downstairs.
I heard him sing in the next room.

patterns.title: see / watch / look at
patterns.explain: see 自然看见；watch 专注观看动态事物；look at 看静态对象。
examples: I watched the children playing.
Look at this picture.

patterns.title: tell vs say vs speak vs talk
patterns.explain: tell 接人+tell sb sth；say 接内容 say sth；speak 接语言 speak English；talk 强调交谈 talk to/with sb。
examples: Tell me the truth.
He said that he was tired.

collocations.en: give sb sth / give sth to sb

patterns.title: have something done
patterns.explain: have + 宾语 + 过去分词，表请别人完成某事，是使役/致使结构。
examples: He had his hair cut.
She had the walls painted.
